Safety Issues.finding: Exterior painted surfaces are failing and flaking, creating potential exposure to lead-based paint due to the age of the home.
The step down from the rear deck into the yard is unusually large and presents a tripping hazard.
The occupant door between the garage and the interior living space is not fire-rated, reducing required fire separation protection.
Animal feces were observed in multiple areas of the attic, creating a potential health hazard.
PEX water supply lines are installed within close proximity to the water heater flue, creating an unsafe clearance condition.
The water heater flue is missing required fasteners at multiple joints, creating an improper and potentially unsafe vent connection.
Service entrance conductors run through or very close to tree limbs, creating a potential electrical hazard.
Sharp panel cover screws, missing romex connectors, and other unsafe conditions were observed inside the electrical panel.
Branch circuit wiring issues were observed, including missing junction boxes and improperly covered connections.
One electrical receptacle at the front of the home tested ungrounded and did not trip when tested.
The furnace flue is missing required fasteners at multiple joints, creating an improper and potentially unsafe vent connection.
Fireplace firebrick grout is loose, missing, or deteriorated in the burn area, which should be fully fireproof.
The fireplace damper lid is missing or not installed, preventing proper control of combustion gases.
Bathroom exhaust fan ducts terminate inside the attic rather than venting to the exterior, creating a moisture and potential mold hazard.

Infestation.finding: Firewood is stored immediately adjacent to the exterior wall, which can attract termites or other wood-destroying pests.
Evidence of animals is present in the attic, indicated by visible feces in several locations.

Evaluate.finding: Furniture, stored items, and household belongings limited visibility in multiple areas, preventing a full inspection of concealed surfaces and components.
Gutters were filled with debris, preventing determination of concealed rust, deterioration, or leaks until cleaning is performed.
Deck structures could not be fully inspected because close proximity to grade and installed skirting obstructed access to structural components.
Additional detached structures on the property were not inspected and their condition was not evaluated.
The sill plate and rim joist were not visible because insulation obstructed the view during the crawlspace inspection.
Interior structural walls were not visible at the time of inspection and could not be evaluated.
Structural ceilings were not visible and could not be evaluated at the time of inspection.
Cooling system temperature drop testing could not be performed due to exterior air temperatures being below testing thresholds.

Minor Concerns.finding: Drip edge flashing was not installed in multiple roof areas, representing an observed improper installation condition.
At least one plumbing vent did not meet required clearance distances for the area.
Gutters contained significant debris accumulation and require cleaning to maintain proper drainage performance.
Finish paint on exterior wall cladding, wood trim, and soffits is deteriorated and peeling in multiple areas.
Brick veneer is in direct contact with soil or landscaping materials in multiple exterior locations.
Brick veneer is missing visible drainage holes or drainage openings are obstructed along the base and below window areas.
Exterior light fixtures are not sealed tightly against the wall surfaces.
Brick veneer shows signs of light cracking in multiple exterior wall areas.
The front concrete porch shows light cracking in at least one location.
A concrete pad at the rear entry garage door is cracked.
The asphalt driveway contains several small cracks and areas of surface deterioration.
The rear yard grading slopes toward the home and does not direct water away from the foundation.
Biological growth is visible on the front soffit surfaces in at least one location.
A rear soffit rake board shows visible deterioration.
Moisture staining is present on the rear soffit, indicating prior moisture exposure.
The garage ceiling does not provide proper air separation from the attic space.
The garage floor contains multiple small cracks throughout the slab surface.
The rear entry garage door allows visible daylight through when closed.
Cracked or deteriorated grout is present on tiled walls in the hall bathroom.
Interior flooring shows delamination and separation in multiple locations.
Interior floors slope noticeably in several areas of the home.
Sealant is missing or inadequate along the shower floor and threshold in the owner’s bathroom.
Bedroom and bathroom doors are missing functional door stoppers or installed stoppers do not operate properly.
One set of bedroom closet doors does not close completely.
A visible gap is present between the window frame and sill in the laundry room.
An additional support post was installed in the crawlspace and appears to be a temporary support rather than a permanent installation.
Moisture staining is present on attic framing members in multiple locations.
Signs of rafter spread are visible along the attic ridge in several areas.
An exterior hose bib at the front of the home is missing its operating knob and is not functional.
Hot water temperature at the kitchen sink is reversed, with hot water operating when the lever is down.
The tub spout and control handle at the hall bathroom are loose at the wall.
Multiple plumbing supply line materials are connected together in the crawlspace, requiring periodic monitoring.
The toilet at the owner’s bathroom is loose at the floor and may require reseating.
Temporary repair couplings are present on several plumbing waste lines in the crawlspace.
At least two plumbing waste lines in the crawlspace appear to have an improper slope.
The main water shutoff valve location could not be identified during the inspection.
Several three-prong electrical outlets are loose within the wall in various interior locations.
Smoke detectors should be tested at common hallway locations upon moving into the home.
No recent service records or service labels were observed on the furnace system.
The exterior air conditioning condenser unit is dirty and requires cleaning.
One handle on the fireplace doors is loose and does not operate securely.
Attic insulation levels are light and inconsistent in most areas throughout the attic.
The crawlspace vapor barrier is missing in multiple areas, reducing protection against ground moisture intrusion.
Dryer vent maintenance is recommended periodically to reduce lint accumulation and improve airflow efficiency.
The whole-house attic fan is inoperable and no controlling switch was identified during the inspection.

Prepare.finding: The water heater is old and at or beyond its expected service life.
The furnace is at or near the manufacturer’s estimated end of service life.
